heading: What "Unless" Does
body: **Unless** can be translated by the disjunctive method as a standard disjunction. In this method, the word "unless" is replaced with logical "or."

examples.text: "The game will be played unless it rains" becomes a disjunction.
"The porch light stays on unless the power fails" becomes a disjunction.

note: This lesson uses only the disjunctive method.

heading: Disjunctive Method
body: The **disjunctive method** translates "$P$ unless $Q$" as $P \vee Q$. Replace "unless" with $\vee$. Keep the two statement parts.

examples.text: "The game will be played unless it rains" becomes $G \vee R$.
"The game will be played unless it rains" becomes $G \rightarrow R$.

heading: Simple Cases
body: Choose a letter for each simple statement. Then replace "unless" with $\vee$.

examples.text: Let $L$ = The lamp is on, $P$ = The power fails. "The lamp is on unless the power fails" becomes $L \vee P$.
Let $B$ = The bus arrives, $S$ = The street is blocked. "The bus arrives unless the street is blocked" becomes $B \vee S$.
Let $D$ = The door opens, $K$ = The key is lost. "The door opens unless the key is lost" becomes $D \wedge K$.

heading: Either Order Works
body: "Unless $Q$, $P$" and "$P$ unless $Q$" express the same disjunction. Since disjunction is symmetric, $P \vee Q$ and $Q \vee P$ match the same meaning.

examples.text: "Unless it rains, the game will be played" can be written as $R \vee G$.
"The game will be played unless it rains" can be written as $G \vee R$.

note: The order may change, but the connective stays $\vee$.

heading: Do Not Overbuild
body: A common mistake is adding extra words or operators that are not part of the disjunctive method. In this lesson, "unless" is translated directly as $\vee$.

examples.text: "The store opens unless the road is closed" becomes $S \vee R$.
"The store opens unless the road is closed" becomes $\sim R \rightarrow S$.

note: Use the direct replacement method only.

heading: Core Pattern
body: Translate "unless" by the disjunctive method as $\vee$. Replace the word "unless" with logical "or" and keep the two statement parts.
